div.note: CONFIDENTIAL
Public Opinion Research Office
Political Division
York House
HQ CCG (BE)
Berlin
B.A.O.R.2
11 October 48
Special Report No. 718.

After the breakdown of the Moscow negotiations between the three Western Powers and the U.S.S.R. in September 48, 13 Fieldworkers interviewed 442 Berlin-ers during the period 28-30 September 48 on the following questions :- hhh Question 1: What do you think of the decision of the Western Powers to submit the Berlin question to the Security Council of UNO?
442 interviewed. (224 Men and 218 Women)
Number Percentage
1. Welcome the decision and look upon it as a last attempt to achieve a peaceful settlement 318 72%
2. Did not think much of it 68 15%
3. Had no opinion 56 13% 442 100%
Some of the answers given: -
Locksmith (48): "This is the only measure to adopt and should have adopted long ago."
No profession (male 19): "This is right as the Americans and British cannot put up with the Russian attitude forever."
Housewife (38): "Up till now UNO has had, no success whatsoever. Do not think it is, of any use."
Post office employee (a): "Absolute nonsense, they only want to gain time and Russia will not be forced by UNO." hhh Question 2: Are you in agreement?
442 interviewed.
Number Percentage
a) because they are sure a peaceful solution will be found 24 5%
b) because they expect the whole world through UNO to pass moral judgement on the behavior of the USSR 78 18%
c) because they see in UNO chance of coming to agreement on the Berlin question and consider UNO to be the only body competent to deal with the problem 129 29%
d) because it is the last chance of averting a war which would otherwise be inevitable 110 25%
a) because it can achieve nothing (Russia does not recognise resolutions or nullifies them by her veto) 31 7%
b) because UNO is too weak and has had no success so far 12 3%
c) because they think there will be war in any case 9 2%
3. No opinion 49 11 442 100%
Some of the answers given:-
Driver (21); "Yes, the Russians must realise that the whole world is against them".
Dentist (55): "Yes, only UNO can decide. That is why it has been established"
Master joiner: "Yes, perhaps this is the best way to avoid a war."
Pensioner: (62): "No, because the negotiations - ith the Russians will bad nowhere. Force can only be met with force."
Administrations official (60): "No, I consider that UNO is just as much of failure as the League of Nations." hhh Question 3: If Yes - what do you think will be the result?
341 interviewed.
Number Percentage
1. believe that the decision will probably be in favour of the Western Powers in Germany and thus of Berlin 104 31%
2. believe that Russia will use her power of veto, that negotiations will be in vain, the situation will become still more critical (possibly to the point of war) 147 43%
3. believe that Russia will leave UNO 34 10%
Some of the answers given:-
Housewife (22):."Yes, the Russians will be forced to lift the blockade."
Representative (32): "Russia will leave UNO, or use her veto. The democratic world will impose sanctions against Russia. The result will be war."
